Transcrição Completa: 22 Day 22

 

[00:00] It's day 22. Got a few creative follow-up videos under your arsenal now. Got a lot of data built up on all these people that you're following up with.

 

[00:08] And more importantly, you have a big database bank of those that you have the capability to follow up with now.

 

[00:14] So, if you've been using your email, you have your set folder. As I've said before, if you literally go all the way back to day one, which is just going back 22 days by now in your folder, hopefully you have 2,200 people that you can follow up with or 2,100 people by this point.

 

[00:32] And if you don't, once again, you're going to want to go all the way back to the very beginning, hit your communications, right?

 

[00:39] Follow-ups are very simple. Now, if you're using emails, you do not want to send the same exact message every single time.

 

[00:47] You want to open up a text edit or if you're on a, you know, phone of some kind, have notes where you have three different one-liners such as:

 

[00:56] "Wanted to follow up and make sure you saw this. Would love to get on the phone." These are call to action ones.

 

[01:03] "Hey, had some time. Wanted to see if you were available for a call right now. Had something I wanted to show you that's going to make you some cash."

 

[01:11] "Hey, I just saw something about your website on Google. I wanted to make sure that you saw this. You have any time to chat in the morning or the afternoon?"

 

[01:20] Simple one-liners that you're going to rotate through. That way, you don't flag any of these, uh, you know, mediums that you're going to be communicating through.

 

[01:29] That includes Facebook Messenger, that includes any kind of, uh, you know, communication medium once again outside of text message.

 

[01:37] So, you can copy and paste inside of text, but otherwise, you're going to want to go back.

 

[01:43] Day 22, I recommend that you literally get way more than 100 a day.

 

[01:49] That doesn't literally mean that you're going to be able to fall short and act like, "Oh, Jeremy, I've already missed 400 total communications in the last 200 days. I've fallen short that many times."

 

[02:02] So, if you're telling me I hit up 400 people today, that counts. Technically, I want you to set a gold standard that you're always going to hit a 100.

 

[02:13] But days like today where you're going to have the ability to go one-liner, one-liner, one-liner, one-liner, one-liner.

 

[02:20] Honestly, you should have a goal that's 20x the level of what it is that we're talking about at 100 for a simple follow.

 

[02:28] You should be able to, in all honesty, follow up with all 2,100 people by doing simple one-liners if you spend a solid eight to nine hours doing this.

 

[02:39] I want to encourage you to do something I learned from a high performance neurology book that talked about the 25-5 rule.

 

[02:47] Meaning work hard, high intensity, 25 minutes straight, give yourself a five-minute break, and then after that five minutes, go hard for another 25 minutes.

 

[02:58] Then a five-minute break, hard for 25 minutes, five minute break.

 

[03:03] But make sure you get your numbers up today. Make up for every single day that you've missed.

 

[03:09] Use the creative follow-up that you have now. Use all the data driven follow-up that you have now.

 

[03:15] Pick out one thing out of that entire initial data driven email. Follow up based on one of those individual metrics.

 

[03:23] This is day 22. You do not have the capacity right now to do anything other than stack your cash.

 

[03:30] Now, listen. Hopefully, you're like some of the students who have already gone through the 30-day challenge and exceeded their goals in revenue for what they expected out of the program.

 

[03:41] If that's you, okay? If you've already cashed out a little bit from the 30-day challenge, which you should because that's the whole goal of this thing, you will need to know comfortability is your enemy.

 

[03:54] And if you do not have the capacity to continue pushing yourself, continue operating at very high levels, this should be fun. This should be exciting.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Dia 22 do desafio de 30 dias

2.100-2.200 pessoas para follow-up acumuladas

Meta: 100 comunicações por dia (padrão ouro)

Meta ampliada: 2.000+ follow-ups em um único dia

8-9 horas de trabalho intensivo recomendadas

Regra 25-5: 25 minutos de trabalho intenso + 5 minutos de pausa

400 comunicações de atraso mencionadas como exemplo

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Banco de dados de follow-up construído desde o dia 1

Rotação de 3 diferentes one-liners para evitar spam

Follow-ups baseados em dados específicos do email inicial

Uso de múltiplos canais: email, Facebook Messenger, text message

Variação de mensagens para não ativar filtros de spam

Sistema de pasta organizada para rastreamento

 

ESTRUTURA:

Técnica Pomodoro modificada (25-5 rule)

Três tipos de one-liners de call-to-action

Organização por pastas de email/comunicação

Priorização de volume em dias específicos

Recuperação de dias perdidos através de volume intensivo

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Estudantes já excederam metas de receita esperadas

Foco em "stack your cash" (acumular dinheiro)

Objetivo: cashout do desafio de 30 dias

 

DURAÇÃO: ~4 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo instrucional direto para câmera / Desafio diário
